from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-production-defense-14fex5
# Competency: bookbot-production-defense.
# Case 1070: a large-file migration rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity.
# Changed case: introduce an equal-frequency tie and prove the secondary ranking key is stable.
def evidence_bookbot_production_defense_14fex5(correctness: bool = True, accessibility: bool = True, security: bool = True, performance: bool = True, reproducible_artifact: bool = True, rollback: bool = True, recovery: bool = True, residual_risk_owned: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_14fex5 = "14fex5-5xef41-1070"
    assert evidence_variant_14fex5
    evidence_axes_14fex5 = (
        "corpus14fex5",
        "policy5xef41",
        "revision1070",
        "command14fex51070",
        "report5xef411070",
        "test107014fex5",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_14fex5)) == 6
    gates = (correctness, accessibility, security, performance, reproducible_artifact, rollback, recovery)
    passed = sum(1 for gate in gates if gate)
    if not correctness or not accessibility:
        return False, passed, "quality-gates"
    if not security or not performance:
        return False, passed, "risk-gates"
    if not reproducible_artifact or not rollback or not recovery:
        return False, passed, "release-gates"
    if not residual_risk_owned:
        return False, passed, "unowned-risk"
    assert passed == len(gates)
    return True, passed, "production-defense"

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Passing the final happy-path demo makes Bookbot production ready.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Defend user value, corpus authority, text policy, correctness, determinism, accessibility, security, capacity, packaging, recovery, ownership, and residual risk as one release.

options.text: For choose the regression defense, run one convenient ASCII text, omit corpus authority, repository and policy identity, bytes, encoding, Unicode, offsets, reconciliation, ties, stderr, status, changed cases, tests, recovery, and native transfer, then accept a plausible report as Bookbot proof.
Stop release, localize the cause, repair the smallest responsible layer, and rerun the full changed-case set.
Ignore the boundary result because the primary demonstration passed. That claim does not survive the choose the regression defense evidence boundary.

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Submit the certification defense · Exam Production Defense
steps.instruction:
Write the exam evidence note for Production Defense: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A exam Bookbot team handles case 8804 during a large-file migration rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend an immutable Bookbot release across quality, accessibility, security, capacity, rollback, recovery, and residual risk; preserve original text and offsets beside every normalized comparison key and report exclusion; then replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Defend user value, corpus authority, text policy, correctness, determinism, accessibility, security, capacity, packaging, recovery, ownership, and residual risk as one release.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Submit the certification defense · Exam Production Defense.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Bookbot and Python Submit the certification defense · Exam Production Defense, keep Production Defense connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Defend user value, corpus authority, text policy, correctness, determinism, accessibility, security, capacity, packaging, recovery, ownership, and residual risk as one release.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Submit the certification defense · Exam Production Defense, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Production Defense precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Submit the certification defense · Exam Production Defense, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A exam Bookbot team handles case 8804 during a large-file migration rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend an immutable Bookbot release across quality, accessibility, security, capacity, rollback, recovery, and residual risk; preserve original text and offsets beside every normalized comparison key and report exclusion; then replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Defend user value, corpus authority, text policy, correctness, determinism, accessibility, security, capacity, packaging, recovery, ownership, and residual risk as one release.

For Bookbot and Python Submit the certification defense · Exam Production Defense, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Passing the final happy-path demo makes Bookbot production ready.
